data.title: CarMax GAAP EPS of $1.38 beats by $0.21, revenue of $7.55B beats by $20M
data.description:
• CarMax press release (NYSE:KMX): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.38 beats by $0.21.
  • Revenue of $7.55B (+6.2% Y/Y) beats by $20M.
  • Sales. Combined retail and wholesale used vehicle unit sales were 379,727, an increase of 5.8% from the prior year’s first quarter.
  • Retail used unit sales increased 9.0% and comparable store used unit sales increased 8.1%; wholesale units increased 1.2%.
  • Bought 336,000 vehicles from consumers and dealers, an increase of 7.2%.
    • 288,000 vehicles were purchased from consumers, up 3.3%
    • 48,000 vehicles were purchased through dealers, up 38.4%

data.title: European markets rise, Eutelsat shares jump 22% following French government capital injection
data.description:
Update: This article has been updated to reflect market movement as of 6.52am ET.

London (UKX) +0.44%

UK retail sales fall sharply in May.

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.88%

France (CAC:IND)  +0.51%

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) rose +0.48% on Friday, looking to erase some of the week’s losses, as most sectors traded in green, while oil and gas shares fell.

Eutelsat (OTCPK:EUTLF) shares rose 22% after the European satellite operator announced a 1.35 billion euro ($1.55 billion) capital raise backed by the French state and other shareholders.

Investors, meanwhile, kept a close eye on the conflict in the Middle East between Iran and Israel, and potential direct U.S. involvement.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up less than 1 basis point at 4.42%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point at 2.53%.

The UK's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point at 4.55%.

Coming up in the session: Canada retail sales. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Accenture GAAP EPS of $3.49 beats by $0.17, revenue of $17.7B beats by $380M
data.description:
• Accenture press release (NYSE:ACN): Q3 GAAP EPS of $3.49 beats by $0.17.
  • Revenue of $17.7B (+7.5% Y/Y) beats by $380M.
  • New bookings of $19.7 billion, a decrease of 6% in U.S. dollars and 7% in local currency
  • Generative AI new bookings of $1.5 billion
  • Quarterly cash dividend of $1.48 per share, representing a 15% increase; repurchases or redemptions of 6.0 million shares for a total of $1.8 billion

  • Fiscal 2025 Business Outlook Highlights

    • Company now expects full-year revenue growth to be 6% to 7% in local currency
    • Updates foreign exchange impact to positive 0.2%
    • Now expects operating margin to be 15.6%, an expansion of 10 basis points over adjusted operating margin
    • Now expects diluted earnings per share to be in the range of $12.77 to $12.89
    • Raises free cash flow to be in the range of $9.0 billion to $9.7 billion

data.title: KBR reports TRANSCOM terminates HomeSafe's role in Global Household Goods contract
Everything Blockchain eyes $10M strategic bet on SOL, XRP, SUI, TAO & HYPE
data.description:
Everything Blockchain (OTCPK:EBZT) Friday announced its strategic plan to deploy $10 million into five of the fastest growing blockchain networks—Solana (SOL), XRP, Sui (SUI), Bittensor (TAO), and Hyperliquid (HYPE), positioning ahead of the anticipated institutional crypto wave.

This strategy positions EBZT as the first U.S. public equity to implement a multi-token staking treasury focused on blockchain networks seeking institutional adoption.

"While Bitcoin grabbed headlines, the real money is flowing into the blockchain networks powering tomorrow's financial infrastructure," said CEO Arthur Rozenberg.

"EBZT shareholders are getting front-row seats to the biggest institutional crypto shift since Bitcoin ETFs launched but this time, we're there first."

data.title: Amazon, SK Group to invest $5B to build South Korea's largest data center - report
data.description:
South Korea's SK Group will invest about 7T won (around $5.11B) including $4B from Amazon's (NASDAQ:AMZN) cloud services provider Amazon Web Services to establish a data center in the southern city of Ulsan, Reuters reported, citing the government's Science Ministry.

The AI data center will be the Asian country's largest and will break ground in September. It will be fully operational with a capacity of 100 megawatts by 2029, the report added.

SK and AWS did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

SK Group Chairman Chey Tae-won said he intends to expand the data center's capacity to one gigawatt in the future and make it a global hub that handles local AI demand, during a meeting attended by South Korean President Lee Jae Myung and tech CEOs, according to the report.

He noted that AI was crucial for South Korea's growth.

SK Hynix's (OTCPK:HXSCF) stock rose about 4% in South Korea on Friday, while Amazon's stock was largely flat premarket in the U.S.

data.title: Franklin BSP Realty Trust offers $107M senior notes
data.description:
• Franklin BSP Realty Trust (NYSE:FBRT) has issued $107M aggregate principal amount of unsecured senior notes in a private offering.
  • The sale consists of $82M of 8.25% unsecured senior notes due 2030 and $25M of floating rate unsecured senior notes due 2028, with an initial coupon of around 8.33%. The fixed rate notes will mature on April 25, 2030 and the floating rate notes will mature on April 25, 2028.
  • The company expects to use the net proceeds for general corporate purposes, which may include funding a portion of the purchase price for the recently announced acquisition of NewPoint Holdings.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Black Rifle Coffee taps Matthew Amigh as CFO
data.description:
• BRC (NYSE:BRCC) has appointed Matthew Amigh as chief financial officer, effective July 7, 2025.
  • Amigh brings more than 25 years of experience, most recently as CFO at Ethos Pet Nutrition.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: CRCL, GMS, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged higher in Friday’s premarket trade, as investors kept a close watch on escalating tensions between Iran and Israel, along with the risk of direct U.S. involvement in the conflict.

Biggest stock gainers

  • Circle Internet Group (NYSE:CRCL) +12% - Shares jumped early Friday, extending a 34% rally from Wednesday after the U.S. Senate passed the bipartisan GENIUS Act, a bill that sets regulatory guardrails for U.S. dollar-pegged stablecoins. The legislation, which passed 68–30, now moves to the House for potential revisions before heading to President Trump’s desk, with the White House aiming for approval before August. The bill mandates full reserve backing with dollars and Treasuries, monthly audits, and consumer protections—measures that Sen. Bill Hagerty (R-TN) says will cement U.S. dollar dominance, boost demand for Treasuries, and keep crypto innovation under U.S. control. He added that stablecoin issuers could become the largest holders of U.S. Treasuries by 2030.
  • GMS (NYSE:GMS) +20% - Shares after a report surfaced that Home Depot (NYSE:HD) is also eyeing an acquisition of the construction products distributor. This follows a $95.20-per-share bid from QXO (QXO) earlier in the week, valuing the potential transaction at around $5B. While details of Home Depot’s offer remain unclear, the interest underscores GMS’s strategic value. GMS supplies wallboard, ceilings, steel framing, and other commercial and residential building materials through a broad network of tool sales, rental and service centers, and distribution hubs.

Biggest stock losers

  • Smith & Wesson (NASDAQ:SWBI) -12% - Shares fell after the firearm maker reported weaker-than-expected FQ4 results on Wednesday, citing inflation, high-interest rates, and trade policy uncertainty. Revenue declined nearly 12%, missing estimates by close to $12M, while adjusted EPS came in at $0.20—less than half of last year’s figure and 3 cents below expectations. Gross margin compressed by 670 basis points to 28.8%, and adjusted EBITDA dropped to $24.1M from $37.6M a year ago. Free cash flow fell 12% to $33.5M, while cash reserves shrank by more than half to $25.2M. Despite the headwinds, the company maintained its quarterly dividend at $0.13 per share, payable July 21.

data.title: Trump to decide on Iran in two weeks; Tehran reportedly rushing to export oil
data.description:
President Donald Trump will decide within the next two weeks if the U.S. should launch an attack on Iran, pressuring Tehran to the negotiating table for a nuclear deal.

"Based on the fact that there's a substantial chance of negotiations that may or may not take place with Iran in the near future, I will make my decision whether or not to go within the next two weeks," Trump said in a message delivered by White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt.

The deadline comes as the escalating Israel-Iran conflict enters its second week. Israel struck 100 targets in Iran, including nuclear sites, on Thursday. Iran continues to launch missiles and drones at Israel.

British, French, German and E.U. foreign ministers will meet with their Iranian counterpart in Geneva on Friday to discuss the situation. "The president supports diplomatic efforts from our allies that could bring Iran closer to taking his deal," a White House official said.

To note, Trump weighing a potential strike on Iran has caused division in his support base in the U.S. "We can't do this again," said Steve Bannon, a prominent Trump ally. "We'll tear the country apart. We can't have another Iraq."

Eye on oil

Iran is racing to get its oil out into the world, Bloomberg reported, as satellite images show storage tanks at the Kharg Island export terminal are brimming with crude. 

At the same time, Iran has been driving up its crude exports. This likely means that Iran is directing even more crude into the facility to ensure that it can send as much oil as possible to the global market.

Iran ships 90% of its crude exports from the Kharg Island export terminal, according to S&P Global Commodity Insights.

Meanwhile, Israel could face major fuel supply challenges if it fails to repair drone damage to its Haifa plant soon. S&P said the outage could turn Israel into a net oil product importer for its fuel, and surging imports will test the limits of domestic port infrastructure.
